title: Within-store substitution miscast as nearest-store search
prompt: A retailer chooses one store's assortment before demand. The model sends demand for an unavailable item through a within-store Markov substitution chain until another offered item is purchased or the customer exits. A proposed story instead says customers search for and visit the nearest competing store. Diagnose the mechanism mismatch without adding store-search or location choice.

decision_variables: products offered in the focal store
model_specification.mechanism: An unavailable product triggers modeled transitions among alternatives inside the focal store until an offered product is purchased or the customer exits.
model_specification.uncertainty: customer starting state and within-store transition path

fidelity_requirements: Substitution remains within the focal store
No competitor search or store-location choice is modeled
prohibited_inferences: An unavailable item sends the customer to the nearest competing store
The retailer controls the realized transition path

evidence_needs.claim: The transition matrix represents within-store substitution
Customers visit the nearest competitor after an unavailable item
